questions.q: By what popular nickname is Verghese Kurien best known?
options: The Father of the Green Revolution
questions.explanation: Verghese Kurien is celebrated as the 'Milkman of India' for revolutionizing the country's dairy sector.

questions.q: Kurien is most closely associated with which famous Indian dairy brand?
questions.explanation: Kurien built the Amul brand and its cooperative model in Anand, Gujarat.

questions.q: What was Kurien's professional training before he entered the dairy field?
questions.explanation: Kurien was trained as an engineer, including mechanical and metallurgical engineering, before working in dairying.

questions.q: The nationwide dairy development programme that Kurien led was called what?
questions.explanation: Kurien led Operation Flood, the world's largest dairy development programme.

questions.q: Thanks largely to the cooperative movement Kurien built, India became the world's largest producer of what?
questions.explanation: Under Kurien's leadership India became the world's largest milk producer, a transformation called the White Revolution.

questions.q: The dairy transformation Kurien led in India is popularly known as which 'revolution'?
questions.explanation: India's massive growth in milk production is called the White Revolution, driven by Kurien.

questions.q: In which Gujarat town did Kurien establish the dairy cooperative that became the Amul model?
questions.explanation: Kurien worked in Anand, Gujarat, which became the home of the Amul cooperative and the 'Anand pattern'.

questions.q: Amul is fundamentally owned and controlled by whom?
questions.explanation: Amul is a farmer-owned cooperative, putting ownership and profits in the hands of milk producers.

questions.q: Kurien's cooperative model is widely credited with empowering which group economically?
questions.explanation: His cooperative structure gave millions of small and marginal rural milk farmers a fair income and bargaining power.

questions.q: The model Kurien pioneered in Anand is often referred to by what name?
questions.explanation: The three-tier farmer cooperative structure he built is called the 'Anand pattern' of dairy cooperatives.

questions.q: What is the full name of the cooperative behind the Amul brand, founded in 1946?
options: Kaira District Cooperative Milk Producers' Union
questions.explanation: Amul began as the Kaira District Cooperative Milk Producers' Union Limited, founded in 1946, with Kurien later steering it.

questions.q: Kurien served as the founding chairman of which national body created to replicate the Amul model across India?
questions.explanation: Kurien was the founding chairman of the National Dairy Development Board (NDDB), set up in 1965 to spread cooperative dairying.

questions.q: In which decade was Operation Flood launched under Kurien's guidance?
questions.explanation: Operation Flood was launched in 1970, making it a landmark programme of the 1970s.

questions.q: A key technical breakthrough enabling Amul's success was making milk powder and condensed milk from which type of milk?
questions.explanation: Amul pioneered producing milk powder from buffalo milk, a process not previously done at scale anywhere in the world.

questions.q: Which technologist and friend of Kurien helped develop the buffalo-milk powder process at Amul?
questions.explanation: Kurien's colleague H. M. Dalaya played a central role in developing the technology to make powder from buffalo milk.

questions.q: Which of these top Indian civilian honours was conferred on Kurien?
questions.explanation: Kurien received the Padma Shri, Padma Bhushan, and the Padma Vibhushan, India's second-highest civilian award.

questions.q: Kurien received which prestigious Asian award in 1963, sometimes called Asia's Nobel Prize?
questions.explanation: Kurien won the Ramon Magsaysay Award in 1963 for community leadership.

questions.q: Kurien also received which international food-related prize in 1989?
questions.explanation: Kurien was awarded the World Food Prize in 1989 for his contribution to food security through dairying.

questions.q: Which Bollywood film, made with milk cooperative farmers' funding, was based on the Amul story Kurien helped shape?
questions.explanation: The 1976 film 'Manthan', directed by Shyam Benegal, was funded by farmers and dramatized the cooperative dairy movement.

questions.q: Kurien founded the rural management institute IRMA. What does IRMA stand for?
options: Institute for Rural Mobilization and Aid
questions.explanation: Kurien founded the Institute of Rural Management Anand (IRMA) in 1979 to train managers for rural cooperatives.

questions.q: In what year was Verghese Kurien born?
questions.explanation: Kurien was born on 26 November 1921 in Kozhikode (Calicut), in present-day Kerala.

questions.q: Kurien arrived in Anand around 1949 partly to fulfil what obligation?
options: A government bond after a scholarship abroad
questions.explanation: He was sent to Anand to serve a government bond owed for a scholarship he received to study engineering in the United States.

questions.q: Which advanced degree did Kurien earn at Michigan State University in the United States?
options: A degree in dairy science only
A master's in mechanical engineering with dairy engineering
questions.explanation: Kurien earned a master's degree in mechanical engineering at Michigan State University, also studying dairy engineering.

questions.q: Which farmer-leader and cooperative founder worked closely with Kurien and was first chairman of the Kaira union?
questions.explanation: Tribhuvandas Kishibhai Patel founded and chaired the Kaira District union and recruited Kurien to help run the dairy.

questions.q: Which national leader famously inspired the early Kaira cooperative movement by advising farmers to organize and bypass exploitative middlemen?
questions.explanation: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel encouraged Kaira's farmers to form their own cooperative to escape exploitation by private dairy interests.

questions.q: Operation Flood was funded in part by selling donated dairy commodities from which international source?
options: The United Nations Children's Fund only
The World Bank's direct cash grants
The European Economic Community via the World Food Programme
questions.explanation: Surplus milk powder and butter oil donated through the EEC and World Food Programme were sold to generate funds for Operation Flood.

questions.q: Operation Flood aimed to link India's major milk-producing villages to consumers in which key target markets?
questions.explanation: Operation Flood connected the 'milkshed' villages to demand in India's four big metros via a national milk grid.

questions.q: The Gujarat Cooperative Milk Marketing Federation (GCMMF), which markets Amul, was established largely to do what?
options: Jointly market the produce of Gujarat's district unions
Regulate milk prices for the government
questions.explanation: GCMMF was set up in 1973 to unify the marketing of the various district cooperative unions' products under the Amul brand.

questions.q: What is the name of Verghese Kurien's well-known memoir reflecting on his dairy mission?
questions.explanation: Kurien's memoir, 'I Too Had a Dream', recounts his role in building India's dairy cooperative movement.

questions.q: Kurien's birthday, 26 November, is now observed in India as which national day?
questions.explanation: India observes 26 November, Kurien's birth anniversary, as National Milk Day in his honour.
